meaning: n.泡沫,幻想的计划vi.起泡,潺潺的流
examples: Add the white wine and let it bubble up.
The water in the pan was beginning to bubble.
At the time the telecoms bubble was at its height.
In some ways Google feels like the giddy dot-coms of the stock-market bubble, circa 1999.
Herschel's view of RCW 120, a bubble of gas and dust in space around a massive star.
